# Privacy Preflight Web

[![CI](https://github.com/LucisZhang/privacy-preflight-web/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/LucisZhang/privacy-preflight-web/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

A black rectangle over text is not redaction. The text can still be selectable underneath a
PDF overlay; metadata can still carry a filename or an identity; and sending a document to a
cloud reviewer leaks the original before you've removed anything. Privacy Preflight Web is a
browser workspace built around the stricter standard: **detect locally, review as a human,
destroy the content, then re-open the output and prove the sensitive material is gone — or
block the export.**

Everything runs in the browser with same-origin runtime assets. User files and text are not
uploaded anywhere. [Claim boundaries](#claim-boundaries) below states exactly what that
sentence does and does not prove.

![Privacy Preflight Web desktop workflow](docs/screenshots/privacy-desktop.png)

The screenshot is not a mockup: `npm run test:e2e`
([`tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts`](tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts)) drives the real workspace in
Chrome and rewrites `docs/screenshots/privacy-desktop.png` on each run. The workspace UI is
bilingual; the capture is taken after the test switches the locale to Simplified Chinese.

## The three paths

**Text.** Deterministic English and Simplified Chinese rules
([`src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts`](src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts)) generate candidate spans; the
reviewer accepts, rejects, moves, or changes replacements; redactions are applied in a single
pass against the original string's offsets, so positions never drift; a strict rescan checks

**Text.** Deterministic English and Simplified Chinese rules
([`src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts`](src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts)) generate candidate spans; the
reviewer accepts, rejects, moves, or changes replacements; redactions are applied in a single
pass against the original string's offsets, so positions never drift; a strict rescan checks
for residual values and sensitive patterns before export.

**Images.** Local OCR (Tesseract.js, English + Simplified Chinese) proposes regions; the
reviewer edits them and adds manual regions; accepted regions are **burned into a fresh
canvas** (opaque blackout, or pixelation), and the output is re-encoded as a new PNG without
copying source metadata.

**PDFs.** PDF.js distinguishes text-layer from scanned content and maps detections to page
coordinates. Every page is rendered to pixels, accepted regions are burned in, and a **new
image-only PDF is reconstructed** — the original text layer never travels to the output.
Validation then re-opens the exported file and checks page count and dimensions, empty text
layers, empty annotations, absence of known sensitive terms, clean metadata, and black
burn-in pixels, and records the output's SHA-256 (Web Crypto). **Any failed check blocks the
download.** Fail-closed is the product decision, not an error state.

## Measured, with its boundary attached

The OCR fixture benchmark
([`scripts/run-privacy-ocr-benchmark.mjs`](scripts/run-privacy-ocr-benchmark.mjs), run with
`npm run benchmark:privacy-ocr`) evaluates seven fixed, deterministically generated fictional
fixtures containing 19 expected sensitive values. Two result sets are currently disclosed,

The OCR fixture benchmark
([`scripts/run-privacy-ocr-benchmark.mjs`](scripts/run-privacy-ocr-benchmark.mjs), run with
`npm run benchmark:privacy-ocr`) evaluates seven fixed, deterministically generated fictional
fixtures containing 19 expected sensitive values. Two result sets are currently disclosed,
and both are retained separately pending owner reconciliation — neither is presented here as
the canonical number:

- **The committed benchmark artifact**
  ([`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/ocr-fixture-benchmark.json`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/ocr-fixture-benchmark.json),
  generated 2026-07-13, a synthetic local-OCR fixture evaluation) records **18 of 19 expected
  values hit, 18 total detections, 0 false positives — 94.7% fixture recall and 100% fixture
  precision**. The one miss (a phone number in the Chinese fixture) is listed per-fixture in
  the artifact, and the screenshot above shows these numbers in the workspace's benchmark
  panel.
- **The earlier README snapshot** of this repository recorded a browser-equivalent
  multi-pass-union evaluation of the seven fixtures: **19 of 19 expected hits, 21 total
  detections, 2 false positives — 100% fixture recall and 90.5% fixture precision**. No
  per-fixture report for that run is committed in this repository.

Both are bounded synthetic-fixture evaluations of fictional documents. They are not evidence
of production detection quality, real-user protection, general OCR accuracy, universal
document coverage, or legal-grade redaction; human review remains part of the workflow by
design.

All fixture documents, names, and contact values are explicitly fictional, generated by

document coverage, or legal-grade redaction; human review remains part of the workflow by

## Architecture

The browser processing topology is committed as a Mermaid diagram in
[`docs/architecture.mmd`](docs/architecture.mmd): same-origin OCR and PDF Web Workers, the
review orchestration (`src/components/privacy/PrivacyPreflightLab.tsx`) coordinating the
text / image / PDF labs, and the shared detection core (`src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts`).

Runtime primitives: Next.js 16 / React 19 / TypeScript, Tesseract.js 7 with eng + chi_sim
assets, PDF.js 6, pdf-lib, Canvas, Web Workers, Web Crypto. The `predev` step
([`scripts/sync-privacy-browser-assets.mjs`](scripts/sync-privacy-browser-assets.mjs)) copies
locked Tesseract/PDF runtime assets from `node_modules` to same-origin paths — no CDN fetch
at review time — and regenerates the synthetic examples.

Where to look next:

- [`src/components/privacy/`](src/components/privacy/) — the text, image, and PDF labs.
- [`tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts`](tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts) — the Playwright workflow test,
  which also regenerates the screenshots.
- [`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/) — fixed
  fixtures, recorded redaction results, the committed benchmark artifact, and the case-study
  manifest.

## Status

Portfolio case-study snapshot. There are no releases or tags and no published versioning,
support, or contribution commitments; CI (typecheck, lint, build) runs on each push.

## Relationship to the Mac app

This repository is the web implementation of a review workflow first built as a macOS app (a
SwiftUI client talking to a worker bound to `127.0.0.1`); the Mac app's source is not part of
this repository. The committed case-study manifest
([`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/manifest.json`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/manifest.json),
generated 2026-07-12) records exactly what the Mac-side evidence covers: a development-app
capture against a local worker on `127.0.0.1:8891`, a passing Swift build, and 95 passed
worker tests. That evidence class is separate from the web implementation's own checks, and
this repository publishes no Mac packaging, signing, notarization, distribution, or
compatibility claims. The web workspace ports the bounded review workflow to TypeScript and

capture against a local worker on `127.0.0.1:8891`, a passing Swift build, and 95 passed
worker tests. That evidence class is separate from the web implementation's own checks, and
this repository publishes no Mac packaging, signing, notarization, distribution, or
compatibility claims. The web workspace ports the bounded review workflow to TypeScript and
browser APIs and stands on its own.

## Claim boundaries

- The UI cannot by itself prove that every browser keeps content local; that conclusion also
  depends on network behavior, deployed assets, and code inspection. The architecture makes
  the local-only path the default and only path for document processing.
- Seven synthetic fixtures do not establish general OCR performance, and no output here is
  legal-grade redaction. The validation suite raises the bar from "looks redacted" to
  "re-opened and checked" — it does not replace professional review where stakes require it.

notices.

# Privacy Preflight Web

[![CI](https://github.com/LucisZhang/privacy-preflight-web/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/LucisZhang/privacy-preflight-web/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

在文字上盖一个黑色矩形并不等于脱敏。PDF 覆盖层下方的文字仍然可以被选中；元数据中仍可能携带文件名或身份信息；而把文档发送到云端审阅，会在你删除任何内容之前就先泄露原文。Privacy Preflight Web 是一个围绕更严格标准构建的浏览器工作区：**在本地检测、由人工审阅、销毁内容，然后重新打开输出并证明敏感材料已消失——否则阻止导出。**

一切都在浏览器中运行，运行时资源均为同源加载。用户文件与文本不会被上传到任何地方。下方的[声明边界](#声明边界)一节精确说明了这句话能证明什么、不能证明什么。

![Privacy Preflight Web 桌面端工作流](docs/screenshots/privacy-desktop.png)

这张截图不是效果图：`npm run test:e2e`
（[`tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts`](tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts)）会在 Chrome 中驱动真实的工作区，并在每次运行时重写 `docs/screenshots/privacy-desktop.png`。工作区界面是双语的；该截图是在测试将语言环境切换为简体中文之后拍摄的。

## 三条路径

**文本。** 确定性的英文与简体中文规则
（[`src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts`](src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts)）生成候选片段；审阅者接受、拒绝、移动或修改替换内容；脱敏基于原始字符串的偏移量在单一遍历中应用，因此位置永不漂移；导出前的严格重扫会检查残留值与敏感模式。

**图片。** 本地 OCR（Tesseract.js，英文 + 简体中文）提出候选区域；审阅者编辑这些区域并添加手动区域；被接受的区域会被**烧录到一块全新的画布上**（不透明黑块或像素化），输出会被重新编码为一张不携带源元数据的新 PNG。

**PDF。** PDF.js 区分文本层与扫描内容，并将检测结果映射到页面坐标。每一页都被渲染为像素，被接受的区域被烧录进去，然后**重建一个全新的纯图像 PDF**——原始文本层永远不会进入输出。随后验证环节会重新打开导出的文件，检查页数与页面尺寸、文本层为空、注释为空、已知敏感词不存在、元数据干净、烧录像素为黑色，并记录输出的 SHA-256（Web Crypto）。**任何一项检查失败都会阻止下载。** 失败即关闭（fail-closed）是产品决策，而不是一种错误状态。

## 有测量结果，且附带其边界

OCR 夹具基准测试
（[`scripts/run-privacy-ocr-benchmark.mjs`](scripts/run-privacy-ocr-benchmark.mjs)，通过
`npm run benchmark:privacy-ocr` 运行）对七个固定的、以确定性方式生成的虚构夹具进行评估，其中包含 19 个预期敏感值。目前披露了两组结果，在等待所有者核对之前两组结果均被分别保留——两者在此都不被呈现为权威数字：

- **已提交的基准产物**
  （[`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/ocr-fixture-benchmark.json`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/ocr-fixture-benchmark.json)，

`npm run benchmark:privacy-ocr` 运行）对七个固定的、以确定性方式生成的虚构夹具进行评估，其中包含 19 个预期敏感值。目前披露了两组结果，在等待所有者核对之前两组结果均被分别保留——两者在此都不被呈现为权威数字：

- **已提交的基准产物**
  （[`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/ocr-fixture-benchmark.json`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/ocr-fixture-benchmark.json)，
  生成于 2026-07-13，一次合成的本地 OCR 夹具评估）记录了 **19 个预期值中命中 18 个，共 18 次检测，0 个误报——夹具召回率 94.7%，夹具精确率 100%**。唯一的漏检（中文夹具中的一个电话号码）在产物中按夹具逐项列出，上面的截图也展示了工作区基准面板中的这些数字。
- **本仓库较早的 README 快照**记录了一次针对这七个夹具的浏览器等价多轮并集评估：**19 个预期值全部命中，共 21 次检测，2 个误报——夹具召回率 100%，夹具精确率 90.5%**。该次运行没有按夹具逐项的报告提交到本仓库中。

两者都是有边界的、针对虚构文档的合成夹具评估。它们不构成对生产环境检测质量、真实用户保护、通用 OCR 准确率、通用文档覆盖能力或法律级脱敏的证据；按设计，人工审阅仍是该工作流的一部分。

所有夹具文档、姓名与联系方式均为明确虚构，由
[`scripts/generate-privacy-examples.mjs`](scripts/generate-privacy-examples.mjs) 生成。

## 架构

浏览器处理拓扑以 Mermaid 图的形式提交在
[`docs/architecture.mmd`](docs/architecture.mmd) 中：同源的 OCR 与 PDF Web Worker、协调文本 / 图片 / PDF 三个实验室的审阅编排层（`src/components/privacy/PrivacyPreflightLab.tsx`），以及共享的检测核心（`src/lib/privacy-redaction.ts`）。

运行时基础组件：Next.js 16 / React 19 / TypeScript、带 eng + chi_sim 资源的 Tesseract.js 7、PDF.js 6、pdf-lib、Canvas、Web Workers、Web Crypto。`predev` 步骤
（[`scripts/sync-privacy-browser-assets.mjs`](scripts/sync-privacy-browser-assets.mjs)）将锁定的 Tesseract/PDF 运行时资源从 `node_modules` 复制到同源路径——审阅时不从 CDN 拉取——并重新生成合成示例。

接下来可以看：

- [`src/components/privacy/`](src/components/privacy/) —— 文本、图片与 PDF 三个实验室。
- [`tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts`](tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts) —— Playwright 工作流测试，
  它也会重新生成截图。
- [`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/) —— 固定
  夹具、记录的脱敏结果、已提交的基准产物，以及案例研究清单（manifest）。

## 快速开始

```bash
npm ci
npm run dev        # http://localhost:3000

```bash
npm ci
npm run dev        # http://localhost:3000
```

CI（[`.github/workflows/ci.yml`](.github/workflows/ci.yml)）在每次推送时运行 `npm ci`、类型检查、lint 和构建。本地验证手段：

```bash
npm run typecheck              # 无类型错误退出即通过
npm run lint                   # 无 lint 发现即通过
npm run benchmark:privacy-ocr  # 对 7 个夹具重新运行本地 OCR 并打印一行摘要；
                               # 注意：它会覆盖已提交的基准产物
npm run test:e2e               # 构建、在 Chrome 中跨 3 种视口驱动工作流，
                               # 并重新生成 docs/screenshots/*.png
```

## 状态

作品集案例研究快照。没有发布版本或标签，也没有已公布的版本管理、支持或贡献承诺；CI（类型检查、lint、构建）在每次推送时运行。

## 与 Mac 应用的关系

本仓库是一个审阅工作流的 Web 实现，该工作流最初以 macOS 应用的形式构建（一个 SwiftUI 客户端与绑定在 `127.0.0.1` 上的 worker 通信）；Mac 应用的源代码不属于本仓库。已提交的案例研究清单
（[`public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/manifest.json`](public/case-studies/privacy-preflight/manifest.json)，
生成于 2026-07-12）精确记录了 Mac 侧证据所覆盖的内容：一次针对 `127.0.0.1:8891` 上本地 worker 的开发版应用抓取、一次通过的 Swift 构建，以及 95 个通过的 worker 测试。该证据类别与 Web 实现自身的检查是相互独立的，本仓库不发布任何 Mac 打包、签名、公证、分发或兼容性声明。Web 工作区将这个有边界的审阅工作流移植到了 TypeScript 与浏览器 API，并独立成立。

## 声明边界

- UI 本身无法证明每个浏览器都会让内容保持在本地；该结论还取决于网络行为、部署的资源与代码审查。本架构将纯本地路径设为文档处理的默认路径，也是唯一路径。
- 七个合成夹具不能确立通用的 OCR 性能，且这里的任何输出都不是法律级脱敏。验证套件把标准从“看起来已脱敏”提高到“重新打开并检查过”——在高风险场景需要专业审阅时，它并不能取代专业审阅。

# Margin Control Tower

[![CI](https://github.com/LucisZhang/margin-control-tower/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/LucisZhang/margin-control-tower/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

Margin dashboards usually show you a KPI and hide everything that made it: the grain, the
metric formulas, the split rules, the assumptions behind the recommended action. This project
inverts that. It's a browser workbench for weekly contribution margin where the **data
contract, accounting identities, anomaly provenance, holdout boundary, and scenario
assumptions are all first-class, inspectable objects** — and where a failed contract check
flags the decision output as blocked instead of decorating it.

A hosted version runs on the companion portfolio deployment (external to this repository;
its current state is not verified from here):
https://portfolio-site-nsam734g0-luciszhangs-projects.vercel.app/analytics/margin-control-tower

![Margin Control Tower desktop workflow](docs/screenshots/margin-desktop.png)

The screenshot is regenerated by `npm run test:e2e`: the Playwright workflow test
(`tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts`) captures it full-page from the running workbench.

## What's in this repository

This is the **governed synthetic workbench**: a fixed-seed generator, committed fixture
artifacts, typed contracts, and a Next.js analysis surface that computes everything
browser-natively.

A companion integration on the portfolio's
[`codex/portfolio-phase2`](https://github.com/LucisZhang/portfolio-site/tree/codex/portfolio-phase2)
branch adds a real-data mode on top of this workbench. Those real-data artifacts, methods,

A companion integration on the portfolio's
[`codex/portfolio-phase2`](https://github.com/LucisZhang/portfolio-site/tree/codex/portfolio-phase2)
branch adds a real-data mode on top of this workbench. Those real-data artifacts, methods,
and results live there and are documented there — every number in this repository is
synthetic fixture behavior by design.

## The fixture

Seed `2026071301` deterministically generates **9,360 rows** at
week × product × region × channel grain: 52 weeks, 20 products, 5 categories, 3 regions,
3 channels, summing to 528,367 synthetic orders. Structure that matters:

- The **final eight weeks are a disjoint holdout** (7,920 analysis rows / 1,440 holdout rows),
  excluded from the diagnostic period so the scenario workflow has an honest boundary.
- A **fixed anomaly at week index 42** hits Electronics in the West — 12 rows (4 products ×
  1 region × 3 channels) with elevated orders, promotion depth, returns, and fulfillment cost.
  Those 12 rows sum to synthetic gross revenue 74,346 and contribution margin −10,331.52
  under the registry formulas. The anomaly is *injected and labeled*, so the diagnostic
  workflow can be demonstrated without pretending detection skill.
- Outputs: JSON, CSV, sample CSV, and ZSTD Parquet. The JSON envelope embeds a SHA-256 hash
  of the row array (`rows_sha256`), and every row carries `provenance=synthetic`.

**Ten deterministic checks** validate required fields, unique grain, non-null dimensions,
numeric bounds, the gross → net → contribution accounting identities, split validity, and
provenance. The contract's declared failure policy is fail-closed: on a failed check the

**Ten deterministic checks** validate required fields, unique grain, non-null dimensions,
numeric bounds, the gross → net → contribution accounting identities, split validity, and
provenance. The contract's declared failure policy is fail-closed: on a failed check the
workbench flags the decision output as blocked.

## What the workbench computes

- A **seven-step margin waterfall** — gross revenue → discounts → returns → net →
  COGS → fulfillment → contribution margin — linked to every filter.
- A 52-week trend, category × region heatmap, cost-driver views, and product contributors,
  all computed in the browser from the committed fixture.
- A **promotion scenario with a disclosed rule**: a fixed unit-response assumption, printed
  verbatim in the UI, maps promotion-depth changes to unit changes while return rate and
  unit economics stay fixed. The UI labels this an *assumption*, not a forecast — the
  difference between a scenario tool and a prediction claim.
- Holdout prompts that mark the final eight weeks as held-out comparison, not diagnostic
  evidence.

## Quickstart

```bash
npm ci
npm run dev        # http://localhost:3000 — deterministic startup from the committed fixture
```

With the committed fixture, the workbench control bar reports `10 / 10 contracts pass`.

Verification surface:

```bash
npm run generate:data                # regenerate the fixture from the fixed seed
npm run generate:analytics-parquet
npm run typecheck
npm run lint
npm run test:e2e                     # next build + Playwright
```

## Claim boundaries

- Every row is synthetic; every metric is fixture behavior. The injected anomaly and the

## Claim boundaries

- Every row is synthetic; every metric is fixture behavior. The injected anomaly and the
  scenario demonstrate a decision *workflow* — they establish no real lift, no detection
  accuracy, no forecasting quality, no causal impact.
- This is a static analytics case study, not a warehouse, scheduler, or multi-service
  platform.
- Real-data work — its datasets, licenses, methods, and results — belongs to the companion
  portfolio-branch integration and its own documented proxies and limitations. None of it is
  produced or evidenced by this repository.

## Repository map

```text
margin-control-tower/
├── src/app/page.tsx                                 # route entry
├── src/components/analytics/MarginControlTower.tsx  # the margin workbench
├── scripts/generate-analytics-fixtures.mjs          # seeded fixture generator
├── tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts                       # Playwright workflow test (writes docs/screenshots/)
├── public/case-studies/margin-control-tower/
│   ├── README.md              # metrics and workflow guide
│   ├── architecture.mmd       # system architecture (Mermaid)
│   ├── data-contract.json     # analytical grain + contract
│   └── metric-registry.json   # metric definitions and formulas
└── docs/screenshots/
```

The `data-contract.json` / `metric-registry.json` pair is the part to read first: the grain
and every metric formula are declared as data, which is what makes the ten checks and the
waterfall reconciliation possible.

This repository is a portfolio case-study snapshot, first built 2026-07, with no release
cadence, support commitment, or external contributor process.

# Margin Control Tower

[![CI](https://github.com/LucisZhang/margin-control-tower/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/LucisZhang/margin-control-tower/actions/workflows/ci.yml)

毛利仪表盘通常只向你展示一个 KPI,却隐藏了构成它的所有东西:数据粒度、指标公式、拆分规则、以及推荐行动背后的假设。本项目反其道而行之。它是一个面向每周贡献毛利(contribution margin)的浏览器工作台,其中**数据契约、会计恒等式、异常来源、留出集边界和情景假设都是一等的、可检查的对象**——并且当契约检查失败时,决策输出会被标记为受阻(blocked),而不是被粉饰。

一个托管版本运行在配套的作品集部署上(在本仓库之外;其当前状态无法从此处验证):
https://portfolio-site-nsam734g0-luciszhangs-projects.vercel.app/analytics/margin-control-tower

![Margin Control Tower desktop workflow](docs/screenshots/margin-desktop.png)

该截图由 `npm run test:e2e` 重新生成:Playwright 工作流测试(`tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts`)从运行中的工作台进行整页捕获。

## 本仓库包含什么

这是**受治理的合成数据工作台**:一个固定种子的生成器、已提交的 fixture 产物、类型化契约,以及一个在浏览器中原生计算所有内容的 Next.js 分析界面。

作品集 [`codex/portfolio-phase2`](https://github.com/LucisZhang/portfolio-site/tree/codex/portfolio-phase2) 分支上的一个配套集成在此工作台之上添加了真实数据模式。这些真实数据产物、方法和结果存在于该分支并在那里有文档记录——本仓库中的每一个数字,按设计都是合成 fixture 行为。

## Fixture

种子 `2026071301` 确定性地生成 **9,360 行**,粒度为 周 × 产品 × 地区 × 渠道:52 周、20 个产品、5 个品类、3 个地区、3 个渠道,合计 528,367 笔合成订单。关键结构:

- **最后八周是一个不相交的留出集(holdout)**(7,920 行分析数据 / 1,440 行留出数据),被排除在诊断期间之外,以便情景工作流有一个诚实的边界。
- **在周索引 42 处有一个固定异常**,击中西部地区的电子产品——12 行(4 个产品 × 1 个地区 × 3 个渠道),其订单量、促销深度、退货和履约成本均被抬高。这 12 行在注册表公式下合计为合成总收入 74,346、贡献毛利 −10,331.52。该异常是*被注入并标注的*,因此诊断工作流可以被演示,而无需假装具备检测能力。
- 输出:JSON、CSV、示例 CSV 和 ZSTD Parquet。JSON 封装中嵌入了行数组的 SHA-256 哈希(`rows_sha256`),且每一行都携带 `provenance=synthetic`。

**十项确定性检查**验证必填字段、唯一粒度、非空维度、数值边界、毛收入 → 净收入 → 贡献毛利的会计恒等式、拆分有效性以及来源(provenance)。契约声明的失败策略是失败即关闭(fail-closed):当某项检查失败时,工作台会将决策输出标记为受阻。

- **在周索引 42 处有一个固定异常**,击中西部地区的电子产品——12 行(4 个产品 × 1 个地区 × 3 个渠道),其订单量、促销深度、退货和履约成本均被抬高。这 12 行在注册表公式下合计为合成总收入 74,346、贡献毛利 −10,331.52。该异常是*被注入并标注的*,因此诊断工作流可以被演示,而无需假装具备检测能力。
- 输出:JSON、CSV、示例 CSV 和 ZSTD Parquet。JSON 封装中嵌入了行数组的 SHA-256 哈希(`rows_sha256`),且每一行都携带 `provenance=synthetic`。

**十项确定性检查**验证必填字段、唯一粒度、非空维度、数值边界、毛收入 → 净收入 → 贡献毛利的会计恒等式、拆分有效性以及来源(provenance)。契约声明的失败策略是失败即关闭(fail-closed):当某项检查失败时,工作台会将决策输出标记为受阻。

## 工作台计算什么

- 一个**七步毛利瀑布**——总收入 → 折扣 → 退货 → 净收入 → COGS → 履约 → 贡献毛利——与每个筛选器联动。
- 52 周趋势、品类 × 地区热力图、成本驱动因素视图和产品贡献者,全部在浏览器中基于已提交的 fixture 计算。
- 一个**带有已披露规则的促销情景**:一个固定的单位响应假设(在 UI 中逐字打印)将促销深度的变化映射为单位销量的变化,同时退货率和单位经济保持不变。UI 将其标注为*假设*,而非预测——这就是情景工具与预测声明之间的区别。
- 留出集提示,将最后八周标记为留出对比,而非诊断证据。

## 快速开始

```bash
npm ci
npm run dev        # http://localhost:3000 — 从已提交的 fixture 确定性启动
```

使用已提交的 fixture,工作台控制栏报告 `10 / 10 contracts pass`。

验证界面:

```bash
npm run generate:data                # 从固定种子重新生成 fixture
npm run generate:analytics-parquet
npm run typecheck
npm run lint
npm run test:e2e                     # next build + Playwright
```

## 声明边界

- 每一行都是合成的;每个指标都是 fixture 行为。被注入的异常和情景演示的是一个决策*工作流*——它们不确立任何真实的提升、检测准确度、预测质量或因果影响。
- 这是一个静态分析案例研究,不是数据仓库、调度器或多服务平台。
- 真实数据工作——其数据集、许可证、方法和结果——属于配套的作品集分支集成及其自有文档记录的代理与局限。其中没有任何内容是由本仓库产生或佐证的。

│   ├── README.md              # metrics and workflow guide
│   ├── architecture.mmd       # system architecture (Mermaid)
│   ├── data-contract.json     # analytical grain + contract
│   └── metric-registry.json   # metric definitions and formulas
└── docs/screenshots/
```

`data-contract.json` / `metric-registry.json` 这对文件是最应该先阅读的部分:粒度和每个指标公式都以数据形式声明,这正是十项检查和瀑布对账得以实现的原因。

## 状态与权利

本仓库是一个作品集案例研究快照,最初构建于 2026-07,没有发布节奏、支持承诺或外部贡献者流程。

尚未授予任何开源许可证;在作出许可证决定之前保留所有权利。已提交的数据集为固定种子合成数据,由所有者生成。

# Margin Control Tower

Status: greenfield browser vertical slice built in 2026-07. It supersedes the inherited
revenue/RFM presentation as the core portfolio experience; the old Tableau link is prior work,
not evidence for this rebuild.

## Decision

A fictional category manager chooses which weekly category issue to fix and tests a bounded
promotion change before recording an action. The workflow is data -> contract -> metric semantics
-> diagnosis -> root cause -> scenario -> synthetic holdout check -> action.

## Data and license

`synthetic-margin-data.json` and CSV are generated by `npm run generate:analytics` with fixed seed
`2026071301`. It contains no external or inherited rows. The original synthetic fixture is covered
by the portfolio `NOTICE.md`; no external dataset license is asserted. Every row is tagged
`provenance=synthetic`.

The dataset contains 9,360 rows across 52 weeks, 20 products, five categories, three regions, and three channels. Grain: one row per `week x product x region x channel`; the final eight weeks are a disjoint synthetic holdout period. The data dictionary is the generated row schema plus
`data-contract.json`; metric formulas, owner, grain, provenance, unit, and decision use live in
`metric-registry.json`.

The same 9,360 rows are available as JSON, CSV, and real ZSTD-compressed Parquet. The Parquet
derivative is generated and row-count checked with `npm run generate:analytics-parquet` using the
locked DuckDB-Wasm dependency.

## Method and validation

- Ten deterministic data-quality checks fail closed.
- Contribution margin uses the registry formula and must satisfy its accounting identity.

## Method and validation

- Ten deterministic data-quality checks fail closed.
- Contribution margin uses the registry formula and must satisfy its accounting identity.
- The selected anomaly is fixed-seed and explicitly injected; no detector-precision claim is made.
- The promotion slider uses a disclosed elasticity assumption, not a forecast.
- Scenario and synthetic holdout values are compared only as a demonstration of verification.

## Failure conditions and monitoring

The decision output is blocked on contract failure. A production version would monitor source
freshness, contract failures, metric-registry changes, anomaly volume, scenario error, and action
follow-through. This static slice has no warehouse, server, or sleeping external demo.

## Limitations

All business values are fictional synthetic currency units. The slice proves deterministic
analytics engineering and action design, not real commercial impact or causal lift.

  anomaly --> scenario[Bounded promotion scenario]
  scenario --> holdout[Synthetic holdout verification]
  holdout --> action[Category action record]

# Credit Policy Lab

A browser workbench built on one premise: **a risk score is not a business decision.** Between
a probability of default and an approved loan sit calibration, expected-loss economics,
threshold policy, human-review capacity, reason codes, monitoring, and an auditable final
action. This lab implements each of those layers as a separate, inspectable stage over a
fixed-seed synthetic portfolio — no real applicants, no production system — so a probability
is never silently presented as a conclusion.

![Credit Policy Lab desktop workflow](docs/screenshots/credit-desktop.png)

*Screenshots are written by `npm run test:e2e`, which saves
`docs/screenshots/credit-{desktop,tablet,mobile}.png` from the running app. The committed
captures were regenerated after the booked-loan outcome correction and show the current
booked-loan-only outcome metrics (17.2% default rate; 0.1513 raw Brier).*

## What's in this repository

This is the **governed synthetic workbench**: a fixed-seed fixture, a policy contract, and a
Next.js decision lab that computes everything in the browser. It is a greenfield
implementation — it does not load, wrap, or descend from the model pickles in the legacy
Risk-Control-Portfolio Streamlit demo (prior work in a separate repository).

The portfolio also maintains a separate, licensed Lending Club real-data integration in a
different repository. Its data and results are a different evidence class, are not produced or
hosted here, and carry their own limitations (see Claim boundaries below). This repository's
numbers are fixture behavior by design.

## The fixture

hosted here, and carry their own limitations (see Claim boundaries below). This repository's
numbers are fixture behavior by design.

## The fixture

Seed `2026071302` deterministically generates:

- **12,000 fictional applications** across 12 monthly vintages (1,000 each), with
  **9,945 booked-loan IDs** kept as a separate entity so applications, booked loans, observed
  outcomes, model scores, and policy decisions never collapse into one table.
- Three synthetic probability fields per application — raw, calibrated, and challenger —
  produced by hand-authored deterministic formulas (no model is trained or recovered anywhere
  in the browser). Observed defaults are sampled from the seeded probability.
- A vintage-based **6,000 train / 3,000 calibration / 3,000 backtest** split, generated
  reason codes, three synthetic audit groups, and deliberate late-vintage drift so the
  monitoring views have something to catch.
- JSON, CSV, sample-CSV, and ZSTD Parquet outputs with SHA-256 row hashing.
- Only rows with a non-null `loan_id` belong to the synthetic observed loan book.
  Observed-outcome metrics (default rate, Brier, calibration, vintage outcomes) use booked
  loans only; unbooked applications display `not observed`. Across the full observed book the
  booked-loan synthetic default rate is 17.2%.

**Ten contract checks** run in the browser against the full fixture: unique application IDs,
entity grain and split presence, probability bounds, LGD/EAD bounds, threshold ordering,
exhaustive decision bands, outcome-field validity, reason-code presence, non-negative expected
loss, and synthetic provenance. A failed check disables policy recording and is labeled

**Ten contract checks** run in the browser against the full fixture: unique application IDs,
entity grain and split presence, probability bounds, LGD/EAD bounds, threshold ordering,
exhaustive decision bands, outcome-field validity, reason-code presence, non-negative expected
loss, and synthetic provenance. A failed check disables policy recording and is labeled
`Policy output blocked`; a review queue over analyst capacity independently blocks recording
(`Resolve queue overflow`); a failed dataset fetch renders an error instead of the dashboard.

## What the workbench computes

- **Expected loss** as `PD × LGD × EAD`, kept visibly distinct from raw probability.
- **Policy bands**: approve / manual-review / decline thresholds you can move, with the
  resulting distribution recomputed live. At the default final-vintage policy (approve ≤ 12%,
  review ≤ 28%, capacity 180) the split is 160 approve / 345 review / 495 decline — and the
  review queue exceeds capacity by 165, so **the capacity gate blocks policy publication**.
  A policy that manual review cannot staff is not a policy; the gate makes that operational.
- **Monitoring**: Brier scores recomputed in the browser on the booked-loan backtest — the
  2,137 of 3,000 backtest applications with a non-null `loan_id` — at 0.151308 raw /
  0.150605 calibrated / 0.150708 challenger; a first-to-last-vintage PSI of 0.428980 on the
  calibrated-score distribution (a drift measure, not an outcome metric); calibration bins,
  vintage outcome curves on booked loans, reason-code summaries, and descriptive group
  slices — none of which is labeled as fairness, regulatory, or production validation, because

0.150605 calibrated / 0.150708 challenger; a first-to-last-vintage PSI of 0.428980 on the
  calibrated-score distribution (a drift measure, not an outcome metric); calibration bins,
  vintage outcome curves on booked loans, reason-code summaries, and descriptive group
  slices — none of which is labeled as fairness, regulatory, or production validation, because
  it isn't.
- **A synthetic audit record** for the final decision, so the workflow ends in an accountable
  action rather than a chart.

## Quickstart

```bash
npm ci
npm run dev        # http://localhost:3000
```

Verification surface:

```bash
npm run generate:data                # expected output: "Generated credit fixture: 12000 rows."
npm run generate:analytics-parquet   # rebuilds Parquet; prints its path with "12000 rows"
npm run typecheck
npm run lint
npm run test:e2e                     # next build + Playwright
```

Startup is deterministic because the fixture is committed; the generator's only entropy source
is the fixed seed, and the fixture embeds a SHA-256 of its row array that the app footer
displays.

## Claim boundaries

- Every metric on every panel is **synthetic fixture behavior**, not evidence about any real
  model, lender, or population. The generator intentionally injects drift; the PSI catching it
  demonstrates the monitoring mechanism, not detection skill on real data.
- No PII, no external credit records, no recovered production model, no regulatory or fairness
  validation, no production decisioning.
- The licensed real-data backtest (Lending Club granting archive, DOI
  [10.5281/zenodo.11295916](https://zenodo.org/records/11295916), CC BY 4.0) belongs to the

- No PII, no external credit records, no recovered production model, no regulatory or fairness
  validation, no production decisioning.
- The licensed real-data backtest (Lending Club granting archive, DOI
  [10.5281/zenodo.11295916](https://zenodo.org/records/11295916), CC BY 4.0) belongs to the
  separate portfolio integration described above and is deliberately out of scope here. A
  granting archive contains booked loans only, so it cannot support rejected-applicant or
  approval-policy causal claims.

## Repository map

```text
credit-policy-lab/
├── src/app/page.tsx                             # route entry
├── src/components/analytics/CreditPolicyLab.tsx # the decision workbench
├── scripts/generate-analytics-fixtures.mjs      # seeded fixture generator
├── tests/e2e/workflow.spec.ts                   # Playwright workflow test (writes screenshots)
├── public/case-studies/credit-policy-lab/
│   ├── README.md            # evidence and workflow guide
│   ├── architecture.mmd     # system architecture (Mermaid)
│   └── policy-contract.json # policy economics + schema contract
└── docs/screenshots/
```

## Status

This is a portfolio case-study snapshot, not a maintained product: no release cadence, support
commitment, or external contribution process is offered. CI (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) runs
typecheck, lint, and `next build` on pushes and pull requests; the Playwright suite runs
locally via `npm run test:e2e`.

## Rights

No open-source license is granted yet; all rights reserved pending a license decision. The
synthetic data and screenshots are owner-generated.

# Credit Policy Lab

一个建立在同一前提之上的浏览器工作台：**风险评分不等于业务决策。**在违约概率与一笔获批贷款之间，隔着校准、预期损失经济学、阈值策略、人工审核产能、原因码、监控，以及一个可审计的最终操作。本实验在一个固定种子的合成组合上将上述每一层实现为独立、可检视的阶段——没有真实申请人，没有生产系统——因此一个概率永远不会被静默地当作结论呈现。

![Credit Policy Lab 桌面端工作流](docs/screenshots/credit-desktop.png)

*截图由 `npm run test:e2e` 生成，它会从运行中的应用保存 `docs/screenshots/credit-{desktop,tablet,mobile}.png`。已提交的截图是在已入账贷款结果修正之后重新生成的，展示了当前仅限已入账贷款的结果指标（17.2% 违约率；0.1513 原始 Brier）。*

## 本仓库包含什么

这是一个**受治理的合成工作台**：一个固定种子的数据夹具、一份策略契约，以及一个在浏览器中完成全部计算的 Next.js 决策实验室。它是一个全新实现——不加载、不封装、也不派生自旧版 Risk-Control-Portfolio Streamlit 演示中的模型 pickle 文件（该先前工作位于另一个独立仓库中）。

该组合还在另一个不同的仓库中维护一个独立的、已获许可的 Lending Club 真实数据集成。其数据与结果属于不同的证据类别，不在此处产生或托管，并带有其自身的局限性（见下文“声明边界”）。本仓库中的数字按设计即为夹具行为。

## 数据夹具

种子 `2026071302` 确定性地生成：

- **12,000 笔虚构申请**，横跨 12 个月度账期（每期 1,000 笔），并将 **9,945 个已入账贷款 ID** 作为独立实体保留，使申请、已入账贷款、观测结果、模型评分与策略决策永远不会塌缩进同一张表。
- 每笔申请三个合成概率字段——原始、校准与挑战者——由手工编写的确定性公式产生（浏览器中任何位置都没有训练或还原任何模型）。观测到的违约从该种子化概率中抽样。
- 基于账期的 **6,000 训练 / 3,000 校准 / 3,000 回测** 划分、生成的原因码、三个合成审计分组，以及有意注入的后期账期漂移，让监控视图有东西可以捕捉。
- JSON、CSV、样本 CSV 以及 ZSTD Parquet 输出，并带 SHA-256 行哈希。
- 只有 `loan_id` 非空的行才属于合成的已观测贷款账簿。观测结果指标（违约率、Brier、校准、账期结果）仅使用已入账贷款；未入账申请显示为 `not observed`。在全部已观测账簿上，已入账贷款的合成违约率为 17.2%。

**十项契约检查**在浏览器中针对完整夹具运行：申请 ID 唯一性、实体粒度与划分存在性、概率边界、LGD/EAD 边界、阈值排序、决策分档穷尽性、结果字段有效性、原因码存在性、非负预期损失，以及合成来源。任一检查失败都会禁用策略记录，并标记为 `Policy output blocked`；超出分析师产能的审核队列会独立地阻止记录（`Resolve queue overflow`）；数据集获取失败则渲染错误而非仪表盘。

## 工作台计算什么

- **预期损失**按 `PD × LGD × EAD` 计算，并与原始概率保持可见的区分。
- **策略分档**：可移动的批准 / 人工审核 / 拒绝阈值，其结果分布会实时重算。在默认的最终账期策略下（批准 ≤ 12%，审核 ≤ 28%，产能 180），分布为 160 批准 / 345 审核 / 495 拒绝——审核队列超出产能 165，因此**产能闸门阻止策略发布**。人工审核无法承载的策略不是策略；闸门将这一点落到实处。

## 工作台计算什么

- **预期损失**按 `PD × LGD × EAD` 计算，并与原始概率保持可见的区分。
- **策略分档**：可移动的批准 / 人工审核 / 拒绝阈值，其结果分布会实时重算。在默认的最终账期策略下（批准 ≤ 12%，审核 ≤ 28%，产能 180），分布为 160 批准 / 345 审核 / 495 拒绝——审核队列超出产能 165，因此**产能闸门阻止策略发布**。人工审核无法承载的策略不是策略；闸门将这一点落到实处。
- **监控**：在浏览器中对已入账贷款回测集——3,000 笔回测申请中 `loan_id` 非空的 2,137 笔——重新计算的 Brier 分数：0.151308 原始 / 0.150605 校准 / 0.150708 挑战者；校准分数分布上首末账期 PSI 为 0.428980（一种漂移度量，不是结果指标）；校准分箱、已入账贷款的账期结果曲线、原因码汇总，以及描述性分组切片——其中没有任何一项被标注为公平性、监管或生产验证，因为它们确实不是。
- 最终决策的**合成审计记录**，使工作流以一个可问责的操作而非一张图表结束。

## 快速开始

```bash
npm ci
npm run dev        # http://localhost:3000
```

验证入口：

```bash
npm run generate:data                # 预期输出："Generated credit fixture: 12000 rows."
npm run generate:analytics-parquet   # 重建 Parquet；打印其路径并带 "12000 rows"
npm run typecheck
npm run lint
npm run test:e2e                     # next build + Playwright
```

启动是确定性的，因为夹具已提交到仓库；生成器唯一的熵源是固定种子，且夹具内嵌其行数组的 SHA-256，应用页脚会显示该哈希。

## 声明边界

- 每个面板上的每个指标都是**合成夹具行为**，不是关于任何真实模型、放贷方或人群的证据。生成器有意注入漂移；PSI 捕捉到它，展示的是监控机制，而非在真实数据上的检测能力。
- 无 PII、无外部信用记录、无还原的生产模型、无监管或公平性验证、无生产决策。
- 已获许可的真实数据回测（Lending Club 放贷档案，DOI [10.5281/zenodo.11295916](https://zenodo.org/records/11295916)，CC BY 4.0）属于上文所述的独立组合集成，并刻意不在本仓库范围内。放贷档案只包含已入账贷款，因此无法支持关于被拒申请人或审批策略的因果声明。

## 状态

这是一个组合案例研究快照，不是一个受维护的产品：不提供发布节奏、支持承诺或外部贡献流程。CI（`.github/workflows/ci.yml`）在推送和拉取请求时运行 typecheck、lint 和 `next build`；Playwright 测试套件通过 `npm run test:e2e` 在本地运行。

## 权利

尚未授予任何开源许可；在许可决定之前保留所有权利。合成数据与截图均为所有者生成。

# Credit Policy Lab

Status: greenfield deterministic policy/backtest vertical slice built in 2026-07. The inherited
Streamlit scoring demo remains prior work only and is not required for this experience.

## Decision

A fictional credit policy manager sets two thresholds and review capacity. Synthetic calibrated
PD feeds expected loss, then a separate policy layer produces approve, review, or decline bands.
The model score is never presented as the final business decision.

## Data and license

`synthetic-credit-data.json` and CSV are generated by `npm run generate:analytics` with fixed seed
`2026071302`. It contains 12,000 fictional applications across 12 synthetic vintages, with explicit
train/calibration/backtest splits, no PII, no external credit records, and no recovered
production-model output. Application, booked loan, generated observed outcome, score, and policy
decision remain separate entities or stages. The original synthetic fixture
is covered by the portfolio `NOTICE.md`.

The same 12,000 rows are available as JSON, CSV, and real ZSTD-compressed Parquet. The Parquet
derivative is generated and row-count checked with `npm run generate:analytics-parquet` using the
locked DuckDB-Wasm dependency.

## Method and validation

- Raw and calibrated PD are deterministic synthetic scores used to exercise policy mechanics.
- Expected loss is `PD x LGD x EAD`.
- Approve, manual-review, and decline bands are mutually exclusive and exhaustive.
- Queue overflow is explicit when review volume exceeds analyst capacity.
- The browser recomputes synthetic Brier scores, baseline/challenger comparisons, vintage backtests, PSI, and descriptive approval

- Expected loss is `PD x LGD x EAD`.
- Approve, manual-review, and decline bands are mutually exclusive and exhaustive.
- Queue overflow is explicit when review volume exceeds analyst capacity.
- The browser recomputes synthetic Brier scores, baseline/challenger comparisons, vintage backtests, PSI, and descriptive approval
  slices. These are newly computed fixture results, not model-performance claims on real data.
- Reason codes are deterministic mappings from fictional input fields.

## Governance and monitoring

The UI separates score, economics, policy, human review, and audit. A production version would
monitor calibration, score/feature drift, queue SLA, override rate, loss vintage, and descriptive
group slices; it would require legal/compliance review before any fairness or regulatory claim.

## Failure conditions and limitations

Invalid contracts or thresholds block policy publication. This slice does not claim regulatory
compliance, real-world fairness, real applicant outcomes, deployed model accuracy, or causal impact.
ONNX scoring remains a future option and is not required for the stable static demo.

  policy --> queue[Capacity-constrained review queue]
  queue --> backtest[Vintage backtest]
  backtest --> monitor[PSI and descriptive slice monitoring]
  monitor --> audit[Policy audit record]

# exam-solver

Self-hosted FastAPI service that solves exam questions with a vision
preprocessor, optional AnythingLLM retrieval, a three-model answer ensemble, and
a blinded Judge. All model calls go through OpenRouter with raw async `httpx`.

## Setup

```bash
python3.11 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env
```

Edit `.env` and set `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`. Set AnythingLLM variables only if you
want local document retrieval.

## Environment

`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`: OpenRouter API key.

`APP_REFERER`: Optional OpenRouter referer header. Defaults to
`http://localhost:8000`.

`ANYTHINGLLM_BASE_URL`: AnythingLLM base URL. Defaults to
`http://localhost:3001`.

`ANYTHINGLLM_API_KEY`: AnythingLLM API key.

`DEFAULT_ANYTHINGLLM_WORKSPACE`: Workspace slug used when a request omits
`workspace_slug`.

`VISION_MODEL`: Vision preprocessing model logical target.

`EASY_CLAUDE_MODEL`, `EASY_OPENAI_MODEL`, `EASY_GEMINI_MODEL`: Easy-tier answer
models.

`HARD_CLAUDE_MODEL`, `HARD_OPENAI_MODEL`, `HARD_GEMINI_MODEL`: Hard-tier answer
models.

`EASY_JUDGE_MODEL`, `HARD_JUDGE_MODEL`: Judge models for each difficulty tier.

`PORT`, `LOG_LEVEL`, `REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`: Service runtime settings.

## Run

```bash
uvicorn app.main:app --port 8000
```

Health check:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/health
```

## Native API

Text-only request:

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/solve \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "text": "Solve 2x + 3 = 7.",
    "force_difficulty": "easy"
  }'
```

Image request:

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/solve \
  -F image=@tests/fixtures/sample_question.png \

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/solve \
  -F image=@tests/fixtures/sample_question.png \
  -F text='Solve the question in the image.' \
  -F workspace_slug=math-textbook
```

The native response includes the final answer, Judge decision, raw model
answers, anonymized A/B/C labels, RAG citations, vision output, request id, and
non-fatal errors such as a failed answer-model call.

## OpenAI-Compatible API

The `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint accepts standard chat-completions payloads.
The `model` field is accepted for compatibility and ignored for routing.

Text-only:

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "model": "exam-solver",
    "messages": [
      {"role": "user", "content": "{{TEXT}}"}
    ]
  }'
```

Base64 image data URL:

```bash
IMAGE_DATA=$(base64 -i tests/fixtures/sample_question.png)
curl -s http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d "{
    \"model\": \"exam-solver\",
    \"messages\": [
      {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": [
        {\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"Solve this.\"},
        {\"type\": \"image_url\", \"image_url\": {
          \"url\": \"data:image/png;base64,$IMAGE_DATA\"
        }}
      ]}
    ]
  }"
```

## Custom Provider Registration

In any UI that supports OpenAI-compatible custom providers:

Base URL:

```text
http://localhost:8000/v1
```

Chat completions path:

```text
/chat/completions
```

API key:

```text
any non-empty placeholder if the UI requires one
```

cURL template:

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \

```bash
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "model": "exam-solver",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "{{TEXT}}"}]
  }'
```

## CLI

```bash
python cli.py --text "Solve 2x + 3 = 7." --difficulty easy
python cli.py --image tests/fixtures/sample_question.png --workspace math-textbook
```

## Logging

Each solve request emits one JSON log line with request id, stage latencies,
model costs, Judge tier and winner, anonymization mapping, total latency, and
errors. API keys are never logged.

## Tests

```bash
python -c "import app.main"
pytest -q
ruff check app tests
```

## Future Work

- Direct-provider mode with a `PROVIDER_MODE=direct` switch.
- Streaming Judge output through the OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
- Retry with backoff on transient OpenRouter 5xx errors.
- Per-request budget caps based on reported usage cost.

"""Pydantic request and response schemas."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Literal

from pydantic import AliasChoices, BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, model_validator

Difficulty = Literal["easy", "hard"]
Winner = Literal["A", "B", "C", "synthesis"]


class VisionRegion(BaseModel):
    caption: str = ""
    image_base64: str = Field(
        default="",
        validation_alias=AliasChoices("image_base64", "base64"),
    )

    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)


class Citation(BaseModel):
    source: str = ""
    snippet: str = ""


class SolveRequest(BaseModel):
    image_base64: str | None = None
    text: str | None = None
    workspace_slug: str | None = None
    force_difficulty: Difficulty | None = None
    judge_model: str | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def require_text_or_image(self) -> "SolveRequest":
        if not (self.text and self.text.strip()) and not self.image_base64:
            raise ValueError("At least one of image_base64 or text is required.")
        return self


class VisionResult(BaseModel):
    question_text: str
    image_regions: list[VisionRegion] = Field(default_factory=list)
    difficulty: Difficulty
    reasoning: str = ""
    latency_ms: int = 0
    model_used: str | None = None
    tokens_in: int = 0
    tokens_out: int = 0
    cost_usd: float | None = None


class RagResult(BaseModel):
    workspace: str | None = None
    used: bool = False
    citations: list[Citation] = Field(default_factory=list)
    text_response: str = ""
    latency_ms: int = 0
    error: str | None = None


class ModelRun(BaseModel):
    slug: str

class ModelRun(BaseModel):
    slug: str
    label: str | None = None
    answer: str
    latency_ms: int = 0
    tokens_in: int = 0
    tokens_out: int = 0
    cost_usd: float | None = None
    failed: bool = False
    error: str | None = None


class JudgeResult(BaseModel):
    final_answer: str
    winner: Winner
    reasoning: str = ""
    citations: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    model_used: str
    tier: str
    latency_ms: int = 0
    tokens_in: int = 0
    tokens_out: int = 0
    cost_usd: float | None = None


class JudgeResponse(BaseModel):
    winner: Winner
    reasoning: str = ""
    model_used: str
    tier: str
    final_answer: str
    citations: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    latency_ms: int = 0
    cost_usd: float | None = None


class RagResponse(BaseModel):
    workspace: str | None = None
    used: bool
    citations: list[Citation] = Field(default_factory=list)
    error: str | None = None


class VisionResponse(BaseModel):
    ocr_text: str
    regions: list[VisionRegion] = Field(default_factory=list)
    reasoning: str = ""
    model_used: str | None = None
    latency_ms: int = 0
    cost_usd: float | None = None


class SolveResponse(BaseModel):
    final_answer: str
    difficulty: Difficulty
    judge: JudgeResponse
    models: list[ModelRun]
    rag: RagResponse
    vision: VisionResponse
    request_id: str
    errors: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class OpenAIMessage(BaseModel):
    role: str
    content: Any

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")


class OpenAIChatCompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    model: str | None = None
    messages: list[OpenAIMessage]

class OpenAIChatCompletionRequest(BaseModel):
    model: str | None = None
    messages: list[OpenAIMessage]
    temperature: float | None = None
    max_tokens: int | None = None
    stream: bool | None = False
    workspace_slug: str | None = None
    force_difficulty: Difficulty | None = None
    judge_model: str | None = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="allow")

"""End-to-end pipeline orchestration."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import random
import time
import uuid
from datetime import datetime
from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable

from app.pipeline import answer, judge, rag, vision
from app.pipeline.archive import archive_question_after_response
from app.schemas import (
    JudgeResponse,
    ModelRun,
    RagResponse,
    SolveRequest,
    SolveResponse,
    VisionResponse,
)

LOGGER = logging.getLogger("exam_solver.request")
LABELS = ("A", "B", "C")
ProgressCallback = Callable[[str], Awaitable[None]]


class PipelineError(RuntimeError):
    def __init__(self, status_code: int, detail: str, request_id: str) -> None:
        super().__init__(detail)
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.detail = detail
        self.request_id = request_id


def anonymize_answers(
    model_runs: list[ModelRun],
    *,
    rng: random.Random | random.SystemRandom | None = None,
) -> tuple[list[ModelRun], dict[str, str], dict[str, str]]:
    shuffler = rng or random.SystemRandom()
    shuffled = list(model_runs)
    shuffler.shuffle(shuffled)

    labeled: list[ModelRun] = []
    mapping: dict[str, str] = {}
    anonymous_answers: dict[str, str] = {}
    for label, model_run in zip(LABELS, shuffled, strict=False):
        labeled_run = model_run.model_copy(update={"label": label})
        labeled.append(labeled_run)
        mapping[label] = model_run.slug
        anonymous_answers[label] = model_run.answer
    return labeled, mapping, anonymous_answers


def _log(payload: dict) -> None:
    LOGGER.info(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=True, sort_keys=True))

def _log(payload: dict) -> None:
    LOGGER.info(json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=True, sort_keys=True))


def _model_log(models: list[ModelRun]) -> list[dict]:
    return [
        {
            "label": model.label,
            "slug": model.slug,
            "latency_ms": model.latency_ms,
            "cost_usd": model.cost_usd,
            "tokens_in": model.tokens_in,
            "tokens_out": model.tokens_out,
            "failed": model.failed,
            "error": model.error,
        }
        for model in models
    ]


async def _emit_progress(
    progress_callback: ProgressCallback | None,
    stage: str,
) -> None:
    if progress_callback is not None:
        await progress_callback(stage)


async def solve(
    request: SolveRequest,
    *,
    progress_callback: ProgressCallback | None = None,
) -> SolveResponse:
    request_id = f"req_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
    started = time.perf_counter()
    errors: list[str] = []
    log_payload: dict = {
        "event": "solve",
        "request_id": request_id,
        "errors": errors,
    }

    try:
        vision_result = await vision.preprocess(
            image_base64=request.image_base64,
            text=request.text,
            force_difficulty=request.force_difficulty,
        )
        log_payload["vision"] = {
            "latency_ms": vision_result.latency_ms,
            "model_used": vision_result.model_used,
            "cost_usd": vision_result.cost_usd,
        }
        await _emit_progress(progress_callback, "vision")

        rag_result = await rag.retrieve(
            question_text=vision_result.question_text,
            workspace_slug=request.workspace_slug,

rag_result = await rag.retrieve(
            question_text=vision_result.question_text,
            workspace_slug=request.workspace_slug,
        )
        if rag_result.error:
            errors.append(rag_result.error)
        log_payload["rag"] = {
            "latency_ms": rag_result.latency_ms,
            "workspace": rag_result.workspace,
            "used": rag_result.used,
            "error": rag_result.error,
        }
        await _emit_progress(progress_callback, "rag")

        model_runs, answer_errors = await answer.answer_all(
            question_text=vision_result.question_text,
            image_regions=vision_result.image_regions,
            rag_context=rag_result,
            difficulty=vision_result.difficulty,
        )
        await _emit_progress(progress_callback, "answers")
        errors.extend(answer_errors)
        successful_model_runs = [model for model in model_runs if not model.failed]
        failed_model_runs = [model for model in model_runs if model.failed]
        if not successful_model_runs:
            raise PipelineError(
                502,
                "All answer models failed: " + "; ".join(answer_errors),
                request_id,
            )

        labeled_models, mapping, anonymous_answers = anonymize_answers(
            successful_model_runs,
        )
        response_models = [*labeled_models, *failed_model_runs]
        log_payload["models"] = _model_log(response_models)
        log_payload["anonymization_mapping"] = mapping

        judge_result = await judge.evaluate(
            question_text=vision_result.question_text,
            image_regions=vision_result.image_regions,

judge_result = await judge.evaluate(
            question_text=vision_result.question_text,
            image_regions=vision_result.image_regions,
            rag_context=rag_result,
            anonymous_answers=anonymous_answers,
            difficulty=vision_result.difficulty,
            judge_model_override=request.judge_model,
        )
        log_payload["judge"] = {
            "latency_ms": judge_result.latency_ms,
            "tier": judge_result.tier,
            "model_used": judge_result.model_used,
            "cost_usd": judge_result.cost_usd,
            "winner": judge_result.winner,
        }
        total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - started) * 1000)
        log_payload["total_latency_ms"] = total_latency_ms
        log_payload["difficulty"] = vision_result.difficulty
        log_payload["status"] = "ok"
        _log(log_payload)

        response = SolveResponse(
            final_answer=judge_result.final_answer,
            difficulty=vision_result.difficulty,
            judge=JudgeResponse(
                winner=judge_result.winner,
                reasoning=judge_result.reasoning,
                model_used=judge_result.model_used,
                tier=judge_result.tier,
                final_answer=judge_result.final_answer,
                citations=judge_result.citations,
                latency_ms=judge_result.latency_ms,
                cost_usd=judge_result.cost_usd,
            ),
            models=response_models,
            rag=RagResponse(
                workspace=rag_result.workspace,
                used=rag_result.used,
                citations=rag_result.citations,

rag=RagResponse(
                workspace=rag_result.workspace,
                used=rag_result.used,
                citations=rag_result.citations,
                error=rag_result.error,
            ),
            vision=VisionResponse(
                ocr_text=vision_result.question_text,
                regions=vision_result.image_regions,
                reasoning=vision_result.reasoning,
                model_used=vision_result.model_used,
                latency_ms=vision_result.latency_ms,
                cost_usd=vision_result.cost_usd,
            ),
            request_id=request_id,
            errors=errors,
        )
        asyncio.create_task(
            archive_question_after_response(
                solve_request=request,
                response=response,
                timestamp=datetime.now().astimezone(),
                total_latency_ms=total_latency_ms,
            ),
        )
        return response
    except PipelineError as exc:
        log_payload["status"] = "error"
        log_payload["total_latency_ms"] = int((time.perf_counter() - started) * 1000)
        errors.append(exc.detail)
        _log(log_payload)
        raise
    except Exception as exc:
        detail = f"Pipeline failed: {exc}"
        errors.append(detail)
        log_payload["status"] = "error"
        log_payload["total_latency_ms"] = int((time.perf_counter() - started) * 1000)
        _log(log_payload)
        raise PipelineError(502, detail, request_id) from exc

"""AnythingLLM retrieval client."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from typing import Any

import httpx

from app.config import get_settings
from app.schemas import Citation, RagResult

TOP_K = 5
ANYTHINGLLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _headers() -> dict[str, str]:
    settings = get_settings()
    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    if settings.anythingllm_api_key:
        headers["Authorization"] = f"Bearer {settings.anythingllm_api_key}"
    return headers


def _source_to_citation(source: dict[str, Any]) -> Citation:
    title = (
        source.get("title")
        or source.get("source")
        or source.get("document")
        or source.get("name")
        or ""
    )
    snippet = (
        source.get("chunk")
        or source.get("text")
        or source.get("content")
        or source.get("snippet")
        or ""
    )
    return Citation(source=str(title), snippet=str(snippet))


def _looks_empty(text_response: str) -> bool:
    lowered = text_response.lower()
    empty_markers = (
        "no relevant",
        "no information",
        "could not find",
        "don't have enough",
        "do not have enough",
    )
    return any(marker in lowered for marker in empty_markers)


async def retrieve(
    *,
    question_text: str,
    workspace_slug: str | None = None,
) -> RagResult:
    settings = get_settings()
    workspace = workspace_slug or settings.default_anythingllm_workspace or None
    if not workspace:
        return RagResult(workspace=None, used=False, citations=[])

    start = time.perf_counter()
    url = f"{settings.anythingllm_base_url}/api/v1/workspace/{workspace}/chat"

return RagResult(workspace=None, used=False, citations=[])

    start = time.perf_counter()
    url = f"{settings.anythingllm_base_url}/api/v1/workspace/{workspace}/chat"
    log.info(
        "AnythingLLM retrieve target workspace=%s base_url=%s url=%s",
        workspace,
        settings.anythingllm_base_url,
        url,
    )
    try:
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(
            timeout=ANYTHINGLLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
            trust_env=False,
        ) as client:
            log.info("about to POST to AnythingLLM")
            response = await client.post(
                url,
                headers=_headers(),
                json={"message": question_text, "mode": "query"},
            )
            log.info(
                "POST returned from AnythingLLM status_code=%s",
                response.status_code,
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            payload = response.json()
    except Exception as exc:
        latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000)
        exc_type = type(exc).__name__
        log.info("POST failed with %s: %r", exc_type, exc)
        return RagResult(
            workspace=workspace,
            used=False,
            citations=[],
            latency_ms=latency_ms,
            error=f"AnythingLLM unreachable ({exc_type}): {exc!r}",
        )

    citations = [
        _source_to_citation(source)
        for source in (payload.get("sources") or [])[:TOP_K]
        if isinstance(source, dict)
    ]
    text_response = str(payload.get("textResponse") or "")
    used = bool(citations) and not _looks_empty(text_response)
    return RagResult(
        workspace=workspace,

"""Judge model selection and structured JSON parsing."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import re
from typing import Any

from app import model_registry
from app.config import get_settings
from app.pipeline.answer import format_rag_context
from app.pipeline.vision import image_to_data_url
from app.providers import openrouter
from app.schemas import Difficulty, JudgeResult, RagResult, VisionRegion


JUDGE_JSON_SCHEMA: dict[str, Any] = {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": False,
    "required": ["final_answer", "winner", "reasoning", "citations"],
    "properties": {
        "final_answer": {"type": "string"},
        "winner": {"type": "string", "enum": ["A", "B", "C", "synthesis"]},
        "reasoning": {"type": "string"},
        "citations": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"}},
    },
}


SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are the final Judge in a model ensemble.
You will see three answers labeled A, B, and C. You do not know which model
produced which answer. Pick the most accurate one, or synthesize a better one
if all three are partially right. Return JSON only matching the provided schema.
"""


VALID_WINNERS = {"A", "B", "C", "synthesis"}


def _extract_json(content: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    text = content.strip()
    fence = re.fullmatch(r"```(?:json)?\s*(.*?)\s*```", text, re.DOTALL)
    if fence:
        text = fence.group(1)
    return json.loads(text)


def parse_judge_content(
    content: str,
    *,
    provider: openrouter.ProviderResponse | None = None,
    model_slug: str,
    tier: str,
) -> JudgeResult:
    data = _extract_json(content)
    winner = data.get("winner")

tier: str,
) -> JudgeResult:
    data = _extract_json(content)
    winner = data.get("winner")
    if winner not in VALID_WINNERS:
        winner = "synthesis"

    citations = data.get("citations") or []
    if not isinstance(citations, list):
        citations = [str(citations)]

    return JudgeResult(
        final_answer=str(data.get("final_answer") or ""),
        winner=winner,
        reasoning=str(data.get("reasoning") or ""),
        citations=[str(item) for item in citations],
        model_used=provider.model_used if provider else model_slug,
        tier=tier,
        latency_ms=provider.latency_ms if provider else 0,
        tokens_in=provider.tokens_in if provider else 0,
        tokens_out=provider.tokens_out if provider else 0,
        cost_usd=provider.cost_usd if provider else None,
    )


def build_judge_messages(
    *,
    question_text: str,
    image_regions: list[VisionRegion],
    rag_context: RagResult,
    anonymous_answers: dict[str, str],
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    available_labels = ", ".join(sorted(anonymous_answers))
    answer_blocks = "\n\n".join(
        f"Answer {label}:\n{answer}"
        for label, answer in sorted(anonymous_answers.items())
    )
    user_text = (
        f"Question:\n{question_text}\n\n"
        f"{format_rag_context(rag_context)}\n\n"
        f"Available answer labels: {available_labels}.\n"
        "Only select one of the available labels or synthesis.\n\n"
        f"{answer_blocks}"
    )
    content: list[dict[str, Any]] = [{"type": "text", "text": user_text}]
    for region in image_regions:
        if region.image_base64:
            caption = region.caption or "Question image region."

content: list[dict[str, Any]] = [{"type": "text", "text": user_text}]
    for region in image_regions:
        if region.image_base64:
            caption = region.caption or "Question image region."
            content.append({"type": "text", "text": f"Image region: {caption}"})
            content.append(
                {
                    "type": "image_url",
                    "image_url": {"url": image_to_data_url(region.image_base64)},
                },
            )

    return [
        {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
        {"role": "user", "content": content},
    ]


async def evaluate(
    *,
    question_text: str,
    image_regions: list[VisionRegion],
    rag_context: RagResult,
    anonymous_answers: dict[str, str],
    difficulty: Difficulty,
    judge_model_override: str | None = None,
) -> JudgeResult:
    judge_logical = model_registry.judge_logical_for_difficulty(difficulty)
    model_slug = judge_model_override or model_registry.resolve(judge_logical)
    tier = "override" if judge_model_override else difficulty
    schema_key = model_slug if judge_model_override else judge_logical
    response_format = model_registry.response_format_for_model(
        logical_or_slug=schema_key,
        name="judge_decision",
        schema=JUDGE_JSON_SCHEMA,
        strict=True,
    )

    provider_response = await openrouter.chat(
        model=model_slug,
        messages=build_judge_messages(
            question_text=question_text,
            image_regions=image_regions,
            rag_context=rag_context,
            anonymous_answers=anonymous_answers,
        ),
        response_format=response_format,

rag_context=rag_context,
            anonymous_answers=anonymous_answers,
        ),
        response_format=response_format,
        temperature=0,
        max_tokens=2000,
        timeout=get_settings().request_timeout_seconds,
    )
    return parse_judge_content(
        provider_response.content,
        provider=provider_response,
        model_slug=model_slug,
        tier=tier,
    )

# Voice in Security: Local Deployment Guide

## 1. 项目简介 / Project Overview

本项目是一个面向信息安全课程展示的声纹识别演示系统，包含本地 Python 后端与静态前端。  
This project is a speaker verification demo for an Information Security presentation, with a local Python backend and a static frontend.

后端使用 SpeechBrain 的 ECAPA-TDNN 模型提取说话人嵌入，前端提供注册、验证、鲁棒性分析以及 PCA/t-SNE 投影可视化。  
The backend uses SpeechBrain's ECAPA-TDNN model to extract speaker embeddings, while the frontend provides enrollment, verification, robustness analysis, and PCA/t-SNE projection.

本 README 重点讲本地部署，因为这是当前最稳定、最适合课堂演示的运行方式。  
This README focuses on local deployment because it is currently the most stable option for classroom presentations.

---

## 2. 环境要求 / Prerequisites

1. 你需要一台 macOS 或 Linux 电脑。  
   You need a macOS or Linux machine.

2. 你需要安装 Python 3.11 或 3.12。  
   You need Python 3.11 or 3.12 installed.

3. 你需要使用 Chrome 或 Edge 打开前端页面，以便使用 `getUserMedia` 录音。  
   You should use Chrome or Edge for the frontend so `getUserMedia` microphone capture works correctly.

4. 第一次运行会下载 SpeechBrain 的 ECAPA-TDNN 模型，大小大约 80MB 左右，因此需要联网。  
   The first run downloads the SpeechBrain ECAPA-TDNN model, roughly 80 MB, so internet access is required.

5. 你需要允许浏览器访问麦克风。  
   You need to allow microphone access in the browser.

---

## 5. 演示前标准操作 / Recommended Demo Routine

### 5.1 每次重新演示前建议这样做 / Suggested Reset Before Each New Demo

1. 先停止旧服务。  
   Stop any old services first.

2. 重新运行一键启动脚本。  
   Relaunch the one-click script.

3. 打开浏览器后，先看右上角状态是否显示 `Backend: online`。  
   After the browser opens, first confirm the status badge shows `Backend: online`.

4. 如果你要做一轮新的课堂演示，建议在 `Robustness Lab` 里先理解两个清除按钮：  
   If you want a fresh classroom demo round, first understand the two reset buttons in `Robustness Lab`:

   - `Clear Lab Results`：清空本轮 robustness 柱状图，并删除 backend 中所有 robustness 相关 attempt 点，但保留 enrollment 用户。  
     `Clear Lab Results`: clears the current robustness bars and removes robustness-related backend attempt points, but keeps enrolled users.

   - `Reset All Backend Data`：把整个后端恢复到最开始状态，连已注册用户和 voiceprint 一起清掉。  
     `Reset All Backend Data`: restores the backend to the initial empty state, including enrolled users and stored voiceprints.

### 5.4 怎么看 PCA / t-SNE 图 / How to Read the PCA / t-SNE Plot

图里不同点的意义如下：  
The points in the plot mean the following:

1. `Diamond / 菱形`：voiceprint centroid，也就是某个用户最终存储的平均声纹。  
   `Diamond`: the voiceprint centroid, the final averaged voiceprint stored for a user.

2. `Small circles / 小圆点`：enrollment samples，也就是注册时录的原始样本点。  
   `Small circles`: enrollment samples, the original points recorded during enrollment.

3. `Larger circles / 较大的圆点`：verification 或 robustness 的正常尝试。  
   `Larger circles`: normal verification or robustness attempts.

4. `Triangle / 三角形`：cross-speaker impostor attempt。  
   `Triangle`: a cross-speaker impostor attempt.

你应该重点看“相对位置”，不要看绝对坐标值。  
Focus on relative position, not absolute coordinate values.

主要解读规则：  
Main interpretation rules:

1. 同一个人的点，应该大致聚在一起。  
   Points from the same speaker should cluster together.

Main interpretation rules:

1. 同一个人的点，应该大致聚在一起。  
   Points from the same speaker should cluster together.

2. 本人验证点，应该靠近自己的 centroid。  
   A legitimate verification point should land near its own centroid.

3. 冒充点，应该远离目标用户的 centroid。  
   An impostor point should land farther away from the target user's centroid.

4. 如果你只注册了一个人，图还是能看，但说服力会明显下降。  
   The plot still works with only one enrolled user, but it is less convincing.

`PCA` 与 `t-SNE` 的区别：  
Difference between `PCA` and `t-SNE`:

1. `PCA`：更适合解释整体几何结构和大方向上的分离。  
   `PCA`: better for explaining overall geometry and large-scale separation.

2. `t-SNE`：更适合把局部簇展示得更紧、更直观。  
   `t-SNE`: better for showing local clusters more tightly and more intuitively.

3. 两者切换后坐标会变，甚至方向会变，这是正常的。  
   When switching between them, coordinates and directions may change; this is normal.

4. 你不应该把 `PCA` 的数值坐标和 `t-SNE` 的数值坐标直接比较。  
   You should not directly compare the numerical coordinates of `PCA` and `t-SNE`.

### 5.5 什么结果算“正常” / What Counts as a Healthy Demo Outcome

下面这种结果属于正常：  
The following outcomes are normal:

1. clean same-speaker 验证通过。  
   clean same-speaker verification passes.

2. `Cold`、`Accent`、`Phone` 里有一两个偶尔掉到阈值下。  
   one or two of `Cold`, `Accent`, or `Phone` occasionally drop below threshold.

3. `Noise 5 dB` 出现失败。  
   `Noise 5 dB` fails.

4. cross-speaker 明显低于 clean。  
   cross-speaker is clearly lower than clean.

这不表示系统坏了，反而说明你的 demo 展示出了“能力边界”。  
This does not mean the system is broken; it actually shows the operating boundary of the model.

## 8. 常见问题 / Troubleshooting

### 8.1 `5000` 端口被占用 / Port `5000` Is Already Occupied

这是你之前实际遇到过的问题，通常是 macOS 的 `ControlCenter` / AirPlay Receiver 在占用 `5000`。  
This is a real issue you already encountered, usually because macOS `ControlCenter` / AirPlay Receiver is using port `5000`.

现在的一键脚本会自动跳到 `5050` 或其他空闲端口，所以通常不用手动处理。  
The one-click script now automatically falls back to `5050` or another free port, so you usually do not need to handle this manually.

### 8.2 浏览器提示无法访问麦克风 / Browser Cannot Access the Microphone

请检查：  
Check the following:

1. 你是否用的是 Chrome 或 Edge。  
   Are you using Chrome or Edge?

2. 浏览器地址栏旁边是否已经允许麦克风权限。  
   Did you allow microphone permission in the browser?

3. macOS `系统设置 -> 隐私与安全性 -> 麦克风` 中，浏览器是否被允许访问麦克风。  
   In macOS `System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Microphone`, make sure your browser is allowed.

### 8.3 第一次启动很慢 / The First Launch Is Slow

这是正常现象。  
This is normal.

第一次启动时，虚拟环境需要安装 `torch / torchaudio / speechbrain`，而后端第一次启动还会下载模型。  
On the first launch, the virtual environment installs `torch / torchaudio / speechbrain`, and the backend also downloads the model on its first startup.

### 8.4 页面打开了但显示 `Backend: offline` / The Page Opens but Shows `Backend: offline`

请优先检查启动脚本终端里打印的 `Frontend URL` 是否与你实际打开的地址一致。  
First check whether the `Frontend URL` printed by the launcher matches the page address you actually opened.

### 8.4 页面打开了但显示 `Backend: offline` / The Page Opens but Shows `Backend: offline`

请优先检查启动脚本终端里打印的 `Frontend URL` 是否与你实际打开的地址一致。  
First check whether the `Frontend URL` printed by the launcher matches the page address you actually opened.

如果你是手动打开页面，请尽量使用脚本打印的完整链接，因为它带有正确的 `apiBase` 参数。  
If you opened the page manually, use the full URL printed by the launcher because it includes the correct `apiBase` parameter.

### 8.5 Robustness 图表越来越乱 / The Robustness Charts Keep Getting Messier

这是因为历史验证点会累计。  
This happens because historical verification points accumulate over time.

请在 `Robustness Lab` 里点击：  
Use the following buttons in `Robustness Lab`:

1. `Clear Lab Results`  
   `Clear Lab Results`

2. `Reset All Backend Data`  
   `Reset All Backend Data`

这样就能在不删除注册用户的前提下，清空旧的对比柱状图和旧的 embedding attempt 点。  
This clears old comparison bars and old embedding attempt points without deleting enrolled users.

### 8.6 Cross-Speaker 分数看起来不对 / The Cross-Speaker Score Looks Wrong

最常见原因是没有先让“另一个人”重新录音。  
The most common cause is forgetting to record a fresh sample from a different person first.

正确顺序是：  
The correct sequence is:

1. 先选目标身份，例如 `demo_alice`。  
   Choose the target identity, for example `demo_alice`.

2. 让另一个人到麦克风前。  
   Ask a different person to speak into the microphone.

3. 先点击 `Record Lab Sample` 重新录一段。  
   Click `Record Lab Sample` to capture a new sample.

4. 再点击 `Run Cross-Speaker Rejection Demo`。  
   Then click `Run Cross-Speaker Rejection Demo`.

    result = ENGINE.enroll_sample(user_id, audio, sample_rate)
    return _ok({"message": "Enrollment sample stored.", "enrollment": result})

    *,

) -> tuple[int, dict[str, Any]]:

    audio, sample_rate = _prepare_audio_from_request_data(
        data,
        audio_bytes=audio_bytes,
        filename=filename,
        content_type=content_type,
    )
    verification = ENGINE.verify(
        user_id,
        audio,
        sample_rate,
        threshold=threshold,
        attempt_kind=str(data.get("attempt_kind", "verification")),
        record_attempt=_parse_bool(data.get("record_attempt"), default=True),
        metadata={"source": str(data.get("source", "api"))},
    )
    return _ok(
        {
            "message": "Verification complete.",
            "verification": verification,
            "audio_preview": {
                "format": "audio/wav",
                "sample_rate": sample_rate,
                "data_url": _encode_wav_data_url(audio, sample_rate),

        }
    )

        return

    print(
        f"Starting fallback backend on http://127.0.0.1:{port} "
        "(Flask not installed; JSON base64 audio only)."
    )

        server.serve_forever()
    except KeyboardInterrupt:

    ) -> None:
        self.model_id = model_id
        self.models_dir = Path(models_dir).resolve()
        self.models_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self.min_enrollment_samples = int(min_enrollment_samples)
        self.default_threshold = float(default_threshold)
        self.device_preference = device

        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._profiles: dict[str, UserProfile] = {}

    def decode_audio_bytes(
        self,
        audio_bytes: bytes,
        *,
        filename: str | None = None,
        content_type: str | None = None,
    ) -> tuple[Any, int]:
        if not audio_bytes:
            raise ValueError("Audio payload is empty.")
        suffix = self._guess_suffix(filename=filename, content_type=content_type)
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=suffix, delete=False) as temp_file:
            temp_file.write(audio_bytes)
            temp_path = temp_file.name
        try:
            return self._load_audio_from_path(temp_path)
        finally:
            try:
                os.remove(temp_path)
            except OSError:
                pass

    def enroll_sample(self, user_id: str, audio: Any, sample_rate: int) -> dict[str, Any]:

        }

    def reset_demo(self) -> dict[str, int]:
        with self._lock:
            removed_users = len(self._profiles)

            self._profiles.clear()

    def extract_embedding(self, audio: Any, sample_rate: int) -> Any:
        self._require_inference_stack()
        prepared = self.preprocess_audio(audio, sample_rate)
        classifier = self._get_classifier()
        tensor = torch.tensor(prepared, dtype=torch.float32).unsqueeze(0)
        lengths = torch.tensor([1.0], dtype=torch.float32)
        with torch.no_grad():
            embedding = classifier.encode_batch(tensor, lengths).detach().cpu().numpy()
        return np.asarray(embedding, dtype=np.float32).reshape(-1)

    def cosine_similarity(self, left: Any, right: Any) -> float:
        self._require_array_stack()
        a = self._ensure_array(left)
        b = self._ensure_array(right)
        denominator = float(np.linalg.norm(a) * np.linalg.norm(b)) + 1e-10
        return float(np.dot(a, b) / denominator)

def _butter_filter(
    audio: "np.ndarray",
    sr: int,
    *,
    low_hz: float | None = None,
    high_hz: float | None = None,
    order: int = 4,
) -> "np.ndarray":
    _require_audio_stack()
    nyquist = sr / 2.0
    if low_hz and high_hz:
        btype = "band"
        cutoff = [low_hz / nyquist, high_hz / nyquist]
    elif low_hz:
        btype = "high"
        cutoff = low_hz / nyquist
    elif high_hz:
        btype = "low"
        cutoff = high_hz / nyquist
    else:
        return audio
    b, a = butter(order, cutoff, btype=btype)
    return filtfilt(b, a, audio).astype(np.float32)


def _generate_ambient_noise(length: int, sr: int, noise_type: str = "office") -> "np.ndarray":
    _require_audio_stack()
    rng = np.random.default_rng()
    t = np.arange(length, dtype=np.float32) / float(sr)

    base = rng.normal(0.0, 1.0, length).astype(np.float32)
    low_rumble = 0.6 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 63.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
    mid_rumble = 0.25 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 180.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))

    if noise_type == "street":
        passing = 0.35 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 420.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
        shaped = 0.85 * base + low_rumble + passing
    elif noise_type == "cafe":
        chatter = (0.4 + 0.3 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 1.4 * t)) * base
        clatter = 0.18 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 2100.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
        shaped = chatter + mid_rumble + clatter
    else:
        fan = 0.4 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 120.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
        keyboard = 0.12 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 1600.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
        shaped = 0.7 * base + fan + keyboard

else:
        fan = 0.4 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 120.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
        keyboard = 0.12 * np.sin(2 * np.pi * 1600.0 * t + rng.uniform(0, np.pi))
        shaped = 0.7 * base + fan + keyboard

    shaped = _butter_filter(shaped.astype(np.float32), sr, low_hz=40.0, high_hz=6000.0, order=2)
    return _normalize(shaped)


def simulate_cold(audio: Any, sr: int) -> "np.ndarray":
    """Pitch-shift and add jitter/breathiness to mimic a congested voice."""
    _require_audio_stack()
    source = _normalize(audio)
    pitched = librosa.effects.pitch_shift(source, sr=sr, n_steps=2.2)
    congested = _butter_filter(pitched.astype(np.float32), sr, high_hz=3300.0, order=4)
    jitter = np.random.normal(0.0, 0.0025, congested.shape[0]).astype(np.float32)
    breath = _butter_filter(
        np.random.normal(0.0, 0.018, congested.shape[0]).astype(np.float32),
        sr,
        low_hz=1800.0,
        high_hz=6000.0,
        order=2,
    )
    return _normalize(0.95 * congested + 0.05 * breath + jitter)


def simulate_accent(audio: Any, sr: int) -> "np.ndarray":
    """Approximate accent variation with a subtle speaking-rate and formant proxy shift."""
    _require_audio_stack()
    source = _normalize(audio)
    stretched = librosa.effects.time_stretch(source, rate=0.94)
    shifted = librosa.effects.pitch_shift(stretched.astype(np.float32), sr=sr, n_steps=0.7)
    body = _butter_filter(shifted.astype(np.float32), sr, low_hz=130.0, high_hz=3800.0, order=3)
    sparkle = _butter_filter(shifted.astype(np.float32), sr, low_hz=2500.0, order=2)
    combined = 0.88 * body + 0.18 * sparkle
    return _normalize(combined)

# ==========================================
# 4. 界面渲染 (带智能默认值的语言选择器)
# ==========================================
st.sidebar.markdown("### 🌐 Language / 语言")
lang_choice = st.sidebar.radio(
    "Choose Language:", 
    ['English', '中文'], 
    index=st.session_state.lang_index, # 这里的初始值来自我们的智能底层检测！
    label_visibility="collapsed"
)

# 当用户手动切换时，更新 session_state，防止系统强行拉回默认语言
st.session_state.lang_index = 0 if lang_choice == 'English' else 1
L = LANG['EN'] if lang_choice == 'English' else LANG['ZH']

# --- 以下正文部分全部换成字典变量 (L) ---
st.title(L['title'])
st.markdown(L['desc'])

st.sidebar.markdown("---")
st.sidebar.header(L['sidebar_title'])

st.markdown(L['desc'])

st.sidebar.markdown("---")
st.sidebar.header(L['sidebar_title'])

xgb_opt = L['model_xgb']
lr_opt = L['model_lr']
selected_model_name = st.sidebar.selectbox(L['model_select'], [xgb_opt, lr_opt])
model = xgb_model if xgb_opt in selected_model_name else lr_model

feature_columns = ['V1', 'V2', 'V3', 'V4', 'V5', 'V6', 'V7', 'V8', 'V9', 'V10', 
                   'V11', 'V12', 'V13', 'V14', 'V15', 'V16', 'V17', 'V18', 'V19', 'V20', 
                   'V21', 'V22', 'V23', 'V24', 'V25', 'V26', 'V27', 'V28', 'scaled_amount', 'scaled_time']

st.sidebar.markdown("---")
st.sidebar.markdown(L['simulate_title'])

col_btn1, col_btn2 = st.sidebar.columns(2)
if col_btn1.button(L['btn_normal']):
    st.session_state.current_data = pd.DataFrame(np.random.normal(0, 0.5, (1, 30)), columns=feature_columns)
    st.session_state.data_type = L['type_normal']

if col_btn2.button(L['btn_fraud']):
    fraud_data = np.random.normal(0, 1, (1, 30))
    # 强行注入异常特征
    fraud_data[0][9:14] = np.random.uniform(-15, -5, 5)
    fraud_data[0][16] = np.random.uniform(-15, -5)
    st.session_state.current_data = pd.DataFrame(fraud_data, columns=feature_columns)
    st.session_state.data_type = L['type_fraud']

if st.session_state.current_data is not None:
    st.divider()
    st.subheader(L['res_title'])
    st.caption(L['res_type'].format(type=st.session_state.data_type))
    
    with st.expander(L['expander']):
        st.dataframe(st.session_state.current_data.iloc[:, :10].style.format("{:.4f}"))

    prob = model.predict_proba(st.session_state.current_data)[0][1]
    
    col1, col2 = st.columns(2)
    
    with col1:
        st.metric(label=L['current_model'], value=selected_model_name.split(" ")[0])

col1, col2 = st.columns(2)
    
    with col1:
        st.metric(label=L['current_model'], value=selected_model_name.split(" ")[0])
        st.metric(label=L['fraud_prob'], value=f"{prob * 100:.2f}%")
        
    with col2:
        if prob > 0.5:
            st.error(L['alert_danger'])
            st.write(L['alert_danger_sub'])
        else:
            st.success(L['alert_success'])
            st.write(L['alert_success_sub'])
else:
    st.info(L['waiting'])

st.sidebar.divider()
st.sidebar.subheader(L['insights_title'])
st.sidebar.info(L['insights_text'])
